name: Cañón del Val, Los Fayos y Vozmediano – circular desde Ágreda
region_intro: Mountain biking around Ágreda offers diverse terrain at the foot of the Moncayo mountain range, providing a compelling destination for outdoor enthusiasts. The region features a mix of unpaved, gravel, paved, and asphalt sections, with routes traversing through varied landscapes. Riders can explore areas within the Moncayo Natural Park, characterized by rich forests of oak, beech, and pine trees. The elevation changes and natural features like the Moncayo Summit contribute to a challenging and rewarding mountain biking experience. Best mountain bike trails around Ágreda The most popular mountain bike trail is Moncayo Loop, a 30.2 miles (48.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 37 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation changes within the Moncayo region. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cañón del Val, Los Fayos y Vozmediano – circular desde Ágreda, a moderate 24.0 miles (38.7 km) path. This route features varied terrain and scenic views through canyons and natural areas. Local mountain bikers also love the Moncayo Summit Loop, a 23.4 miles (37.6 km) trail leading through the Moncayo mountain range, often completed in about 4 hours 4 minutes. Mountain biking around Ágreda is defined by the Moncayo mountain range, diverse forests, and varied terrain including unpaved and gravel sections. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Ágreda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Ágreda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Ágreda?
A: There are over 40 mountain bike trails around Ágreda, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. You'll find a mix of easy, moderate, and difficult routes to explore.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly mountain bike trails in the Ágreda area?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy mountain bike trail available. The region also offers options like the Vía Verde del Moncayo, which is recommended for easy mountain biking and gravel biking, following an old mining railway trail through a lush oak forest. For a specific moderate option, consider &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058249541/canon-del-val-los-fayos-y-vozmediano-circular-desde-agreda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón del Val, Los Fayos y Vozmediano – circular desde Ágreda&lt;/a&gt;, which features varied terrain and scenic views.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking around Ágreda?
A: The region around Ágreda is generally good for mountain biking outside of winter. The Moncayo Summit is typically snow-capped from October to May, which can affect higher elevation routes. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, while summer can be warm but still enjoyable, especially in the mornings or evenings.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails near Ágreda?
A: The trails around Ágreda offer diverse terrain, including unpaved, gravel, paved, and asphalt sections. You'll encounter routes through rich forests of oak, beech, and pine trees, as well as significant elevation changes, particularly in the Moncayo mountain range.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular mountain bike routes. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985076453/moncayo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 48.7 km route with significant elevation changes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986801726/moncayo-summit-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo Summit Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 37.6 km trail through the Moncayo mountain range.

Q: What natural attractions or viewpoints can I see while mountain biking?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo Summit&lt;/a&gt; itself, offering panoramic views. Other notable peaks and viewpoints include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5573920" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Tío Fresquito&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de la Mata&lt;/a&gt;. The Moncayo Natural Park also features diverse forests and water features like the Nacedero del Río Queiles and the Cascada del Pozo de las Truchas.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks near the mountain bike trails?
A: Ágreda is known as the 'town of the three cultures' with a rich heritage. You can explore Romanesque temples such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2683495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Miguel Arcángel of Ágreda&lt;/a&gt;. The nearby town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1755663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarazona&lt;/a&gt; also offers historical buildings and attractions, which can be part of longer routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40314471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarazona – Castillo de Vozmediano loop from Ágreda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Ágreda?
A: The mountain bike trails around Ágreda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 10 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the challenging climbs, and the stunning natural beauty of the Moncayo mountain range and its forests.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging mountain bike routes for advanced riders?
A: Yes, Ágreda offers several difficult routes for experienced mountain bikers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31882126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Moncayo loop from Estación de Ólvega&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 70.6 km route with significant elevation gain. The Moncayo to Tarazona route, mentioned in regional research, is also noted as an 'epic' mountain biking route with substantial elevation changes.

Q: Can I find places to rest or picnic along the trails?
A: Yes, the region offers facilities for outdoor enthusiasts. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5249052" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Sacristán Picnic Area&lt;/a&gt; serves as a gateway to paths through spectacular forests and provides a good spot for a break.

Q: Is public transport available to access the mountain bike trails around Ágreda?
A: While Ágreda itself has some bus connections, direct public transport to specific trailheads within the Moncayo Natural Park or more remote areas is limited. It's generally recommended to use a personal vehicle for convenient access to the various starting points of the mountain bike routes.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking in Ágreda?
A: Parking is generally available within Ágreda town and at various access points to the Moncayo Natural Park. Specific parking details for individual trailheads may vary, so it's advisable to check local information or maps for designated parking areas near your chosen route.
